nodes.name: World Flags
nodes.description: Distinctive national flag features and symbols

items.prompt: Which country's flag features a black double-headed eagle on a red background?
items.explanation:
Red field with a centered black double-headed eagle; one of the most distinctive European flags
The double-headed eagle derives from the 15th-century coat of arms of Gjergj Kastrioti (Skanderbeg), who led Albanian resistance against the Ottoman Empire
Albania declared independence from the Ottoman Empire in 1912 and has used this design since
The two heads of the eagle are interpreted as representing unity between the Gheg and Tosk cultural groups, or Albania's ties to both East and West
One of only a few national flags in the world to feature an eagle with two heads

items.prompt: Which country's flag combines the Union Jack, the Southern Cross constellation, and a seven-pointed Commonwealth Star?
items.explanation:
Blue ensign with the Union Jack in the upper hoist canton, the Commonwealth Star (7 points) below it, and the Southern Cross on the fly side
The Commonwealth Star originally had 6 points for the six federating colonies; a 7th point was added in 1908 for territories
The Southern Cross consists of five white stars (four seven-pointed, one smaller five-pointed) visible from the Southern Hemisphere
First flew as the Commonwealth blue ensign on 3 September 1901, following federation
New Zealand's flag is similar but has only the Southern Cross (4 red stars with white borders) and no Commonwealth Star
items.cardFront: Union Jack + Southern Cross + Commonwealth Star

links.text: Union Jack + Southern Cross + Commonwealth Star

items.prompt: Which country's flag features a dragon (the Druk) holding jewels on a diagonal bicolor background?
items.explanation:
Divided diagonally: upper triangle yellow, lower triangle orange, with a white Druk (Thunder Dragon) across the center
The Druk holds a norbu (jewel) in each claw, representing wealth and protection of the people
Bhutan's name in Dzongkha is Druk Yul, meaning 'Land of the Thunder Dragon'
Yellow symbolizes civil authority; orange represents Buddhist traditions
One of the most visually distinctive flags in Asia

items.prompt: Which country's flag features a green field with a yellow diamond containing a blue celestial globe and the motto 'Ordem e Progresso'?
items.explanation:
Green field, yellow rhombus, blue disc with 27 white stars and a white band inscribed 'Ordem e Progresso' (Order and Progress)
The star positions on the globe represent the sky over Rio de Janeiro on 15 November 1889 — the day the republic was proclaimed
Each star corresponds to a Brazilian federal unit; the flag must be updated when states are created or dissolved
The motto is derived from the positivist philosophy of Auguste Comte
With 27 stars, Brazil has the second-most stars on any national flag, behind the USA's 50
items.cardFront: Celestial globe with stars and 'Ordem e Progresso' motto

links.text: Celestial globe with stars and 'Ordem e Progresso' motto

items.prompt: Which country's flag is the only one to feature a depiction of an actual religious building as its central symbol?
items.explanation:
Three horizontal bands of blue, red (wider), and blue, with a white silhouette of Angkor Wat in the center of the red band
Angkor Wat, built in the 12th century under Khmer King Suryavarman II, is the world's largest religious monument
The flag has appeared in some form since the 19th century; the current design was re-adopted in 1993 after the UNTAC period
Several other flags (Portugal, Spain, San Marino) show stylized buildings or fortifications, but none feature an identifiable real religious site as prominently
Angkor Wat appears on Cambodian currency and is the country's most recognized symbol internationally
items.cardFront: Angkor Wat temple silhouette in center

links.text: Angkor Wat temple silhouette in center

items.prompt: Which country's flag features a single red maple leaf on a white field, flanked by two red vertical bands?
items.explanation:
Vertical triband of red, white, red with an 11-pointed red maple leaf centered on the white field
Adopted on 15 February 1965, replacing the Canadian Red Ensign (which incorporated the Union Jack)
The 11-pointed maple leaf is a stylized design chosen for visual clarity at a distance; real maple leaves have more points
The flag design was chosen after a national contest and Parliamentary debate; Prime Minister Lester B. Pearson championed the change
February 15 is celebrated as National Flag of Canada Day
items.cardFront: Single red maple leaf on white, flanked by red bands

links.text: Single red maple leaf on white, flanked by red bands

items.prompt: Which country's flag features a map of the entire island on a white background, making it one of only two national flags to depict a geographic map?
items.explanation:
White field with a copper-orange silhouette of the island above two crossed olive branches
One of only two sovereign national flags to feature a map of the country itself — the other is Kosovo
The copper-orange color reflects Cyprus's large copper deposits; the ancient Greek name for copper, 'kypros,' is the island's namesake
The olive branches symbolize peace between Greek and Turkish Cypriots
Adopted 16 August 1960 upon independence from Britain; designed by Turkish Cypriot artist İsmet Güney
items.cardFront: Map of the island in copper-orange on white

links.text: Map of the island in copper-orange on white

items.prompt: Which country's flag is the only sovereign national flag that contains neither red, white, nor blue?
items.explanation:
Gold saltire (diagonal cross) dividing the flag into four sections: two green triangles (top and bottom) and two black triangles (hoist and fly)
Since Mauritania redesigned its flag in 2017 (adding red stripes), Jamaica's flag is the only sovereign national flag with no red, white, or blue
Colors symbolize: black (strength and creativity of the people), gold (natural wealth and sunshine), green (lush vegetation and hope)
Adopted 6 August 1962 upon independence from Britain
The current color symbolism was officially adopted in 1996, replacing an earlier interpretation that associated black with hardships
items.cardFront: No red, white, or blue — black, gold, and green saltire

links.text: No red, white, or blue — black, gold, and green saltire

items.prompt: Which country's flag is a plain white field with a single red circle (the Hinomaru) in the center?
items.explanation:
White rectangular field with a red disc (Hinomaru, meaning 'ball/circle of the sun') centered on the flag
One of the simplest national flag designs; the red circle represents the sun, reflecting Japan's sobriquet 'Land of the Rising Sun'
Use of the Hinomaru dates to at least the 12th century; the Minamoto clan used a white flag with a red circle in the Genpei War (1180–1185)
Officially adopted as the national flag in 1870 (Meiji era); legally designated by the Act on National Flag and Anthem in 1999
Sometimes confused with the war flag (Rising Sun flag), which has 16 red rays radiating from the disc
items.cardFront: Red circle (Hinomaru) on plain white field

items.prompt: Which country's flag features a green cedar tree centered between two horizontal red stripes?
items.explanation:
Two red horizontal bands flanking a wider white band, with a green Cedar of Lebanon spanning both the white band and touching both red bands
The cedar tree has symbolized Lebanon since antiquity; Phoenician cedar was prized throughout the ancient Mediterranean world
Adopted 7 December 1943 upon independence from France; the cedar's color was standardized to entirely green in 1990 (replacing an earlier version with a brown trunk)
Red represents blood shed defending the country; white represents peace and the snow on Lebanon's mountains
The Cedar of Lebanon (Cedrus libani) appears on Lebanon's currency and other national symbols
items.cardFront: Green cedar tree on white center band

links.text: Green cedar tree on white center band

items.prompt: Which country readopted in 2011 the tricolor flag of its former monarchy — red, black, and green horizontal bands with a white crescent and star?
items.explanation:
Three horizontal bands: red (top), black (center, twice the width of the others), green (bottom); white crescent and star centered on the black band
Readopted by the National Transitional Council on 3 August 2011 following the fall of Muammar Gaddafi
This flag was originally used by the Kingdom of Libya from 1951 until Gaddafi's coup in 1969; Gaddafi replaced it with a plain green flag
The crescent and star are traditional symbols of Islam
Gaddafi's all-green flag (1977–2011) was the only solid-color national flag in the world
items.cardFront: Black-red-green tricolor with white crescent and star (readopted 2011)

links.text: Black-red-green tricolor with white crescent and star

items.prompt: Which country's flag is the only national flag in the world to feature a modern assault rifle (an AK-47)?
items.explanation:
Five horizontal stripes of green, white, black, white, and yellow, with a red triangle at the hoist bearing a yellow star, an open book, and a crossed AK-47 and hoe
The only national flag in the world to feature a modern firearm
Adopted 1 May 1983; the design is based on the flag of FRELIMO, the liberation movement that has governed since independence in 1975
The AK-47 symbolizes defense and vigilance; the book represents education; the hoe represents agriculture
Since democratization in 1990, there have been recurring calls to remove the gun from the flag
items.cardFront: AK-47 assault rifle (only national flag with a modern firearm)

items.prompt: Which country has the only national flag in the world that is not rectangular?
items.explanation:
A double pennon (two stacked pennants): the upper smaller triangle bears a white stylized moon; the lower larger triangle bears a white sun; the whole is bordered in blue
The only non-rectangular national flag of a sovereign state in the world
The current form was codified in the 1962 constitution with precise mathematical specifications for the shape
The two pennants were originally the personal flags of Nepal's king and prime minister, merged into one national flag
Red is the color of the rhododendron, Nepal's national flower; blue represents peace
items.cardFront: Only non-rectangular national flag (double pennant shape)

items.prompt: Which country's flag displays the Islamic declaration of faith (the Shahada) above a sword, and is designed so it always shows correctly on both sides?
items.explanation:
Green field with the Shahada ('There is no deity but God; Muhammad is the Messenger of God') in white Thuluth script above a white horizontal sword
Among the very few national flags whose primary design element is a religious text; Iran's flag also features Quranic text along its stripes
Because the Shahada is considered holy, the flag is never flown at half-mast and is not used on clothing or other items
The sword is oriented with its point facing left on both sides, so the flag has two differently-rendered sides — one of the few national flags designed this way
The current design was legally standardized by the Law of the Flag on 15 March 1973
items.cardFront: Islamic Shahada text + sword on green field

links.text: Islamic Shahada text + sword on green field

items.prompt: Which country's flag features a distinctive Y-shaped horizontal pall design in green, with red and blue bands, black triangle, and gold and white borders?
items.explanation:
Six colors: green Y-pall running from the hoist to center, black triangle at the hoist (with gold border), red upper band (with white border), blue lower band (with white border)
One of the few national flags in the world with six colors in its primary design, without a seal or emblem
Designed by Frederick Brownell; adopted 27 April 1994, the date of South Africa's first post-apartheid election
The Y-pall heraldically symbolizes the convergence of diverse paths into a unified future
Three colors (red, white, blue) derive from the old Dutch/South African Republic flag; three (black, green, gold) from the ANC flag
items.cardFront: Six-color flag with distinctive green Y-shape (pall)

links.text: Six-color flag with distinctive green Y-shape

items.prompt: Which country's national flag is a square (not rectangular) red field with a bold white cross?
items.explanation:
Red square field with a white equilateral cross (arms one-sixth longer than their width); the cross does not extend to the edges
One of only two sovereign national flags that are square rather than rectangular — the other is Vatican City
Evolves from Swiss military cantonal flags; the red and white cross appeared on the Swiss Federal Charter of 1291
Because the IOC requires rectangular flags, Switzerland uses a rectangular version at the Olympic Games
The Red Cross emblem (inverted colors: white field, red cross) was created in honor of Swiss humanitarian Henry Dunant, co-founder of the ICRC
items.cardFront: Square flag — white cross on red

links.text: Square flag — white cross on red

items.prompt: Which Caribbean country's flag features a black diagonal stripe with white borders running from the upper hoist to the lower fly on a red field?
items.explanation:
Red field with a black diagonal band (from upper left to lower right) bordered by two narrow white stripes
Adopted 31 August 1962 upon independence from Britain; designed by Carlisle Chang
Red symbolizes the sun and people's courage; black represents dedication and the earth; white represents purity and equality (and the sea)
The diagonal band design is relatively unusual among Caribbean national flags
The flag's three colors were chosen to represent the natural elements of sun, earth, and sea
items.cardFront: Black diagonal stripe with white borders on red field

links.text: Black diagonal stripe with white borders on red field

items.prompt: Which country's flag has the most stars of any national flag, with 50 representing its states?
items.explanation:
13 horizontal stripes alternating red and white (for the 13 original colonies), with a blue canton bearing 50 white stars in 9 alternating rows
With 50 stars, the USA flag has the most stars of any national flag — more than twice as many as the second-place Brazil (27 stars)
The current 50-star design was adopted 4 July 1960, after Hawaii became the 50th state in 1959
The flag has been updated 27 times; the first official flag (1777) had 13 stars
Alternate name 'Old Glory' was given by sea captain William Driver in 1831; 'Stars and Stripes' is the other common nickname
items.cardFront: 50 stars — most on any national flag

items.prompt: Which country's flag is square (like Switzerland's), with vertical gold and white halves and the papal coat of arms?
items.explanation:
Square flag vertically divided: gold (hoist) and white (fly), with the papal tiara and crossed keys of Saint Peter on the white half
One of only two sovereign national flags that are square rather than rectangular — the other is Switzerland
Adopted 7 June 1929 following the Lateran Treaty, which established Vatican City as an independent state
The crossed keys (gold and silver) symbolize the keys to heaven entrusted to Saint Peter
Vatican City, at approximately 44 hectares (110 acres), is the smallest sovereign state in the world
items.cardFront: Square flag — gold and white halves with papal coat of arms

links.text: Square flag — gold and white halves with papal coat of arms
